title: Fermented Silky Croatian Doner Broth
description: A silky, fermented broth inspired by Croatian doner, crafted with delicate fish for a pescatarian-friendly date night.

ingredients.name: Sea bass fillets
ingredients.notes: skin removed, cut into bite‑sized pieces

ingredients.name: Kombucha
ingredients.notes: plain, unflavored, to add gentle acidity

instructions: 1. Pat the sea bass fillets dry and season lightly with sea salt and black pepper.
2. In a medium saucepan, bring the fish stock to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
3. Add the seasoned fillets to the stock and simmer for 8–10 minutes, until the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
4. Remove the fillets with a slotted spoon and set aside. Strain the broth to remove any small fish pieces or bones.
5. Return the strained broth to the saucepan and bring back to a low simmer.
6. Stir in the coconut milk, fermented soy sauce, kombucha, smoked paprika, dried dill, and lemon zest.
7. Simmer the mixture for an additional 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the broth to thicken slightly.
8. Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt or pepper if needed.
9. Return the flaked fish to the broth, gently warming it through.
10. Ladle the broth into bowls, ensuring each spoonful has a generous portion of fish and any remaining herbs.
11. Serve immediately, perhaps with a side of crusty bread or a light salad for a complete date‑night meal.
